items.briefing:
A job scam is circulating that targets marketing professionals by impersonating well-known brands on job platforms. The scam aims to steal victims' Google account credentials, so be cautious of unsolicited job offers asking you to sign in with Google or share login details. Separately, Australian security leaders are being reminded to focus on the basics — patching systems, managing access, and reviewing security fundamentals — as AI tools are making it faster and easier for attackers to find and exploit vulnerabilities.

**Practical tip:** Never click "Sign in with Google" through a link in a job offer email — go directly to the employer's official website instead.

featured.title: 'BusySnake' Infostealer Slithers Into Critical Infrastructure Networks
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure networks compromised by infostealer poses direct threat to Australian essential services.
